reason: Bram Stoker’s Dracula brings a dark folk-tale feel to a superstitious village beset by an outsider
The Innocents drapes a remote estate in eerie atmosphere, where off-kilter dread seeps into every corner
Courage Under Fire traps soldiers under an unseen threat, their superstitions feeding an earthy unease
U.S. Marshals slips into a small town’s fearful folklore, damp and primal in every shadowed alley
Nightwatch sets its saga in a hospital’s off-kilter halls, where natural laws feel slightly askew
Police Story 4: Project S turns a Hong Kong backlot into a superstitious fortress of ritual and paranoia
Flintstones, The grafts stone-age superstition onto a remote village, earthy and unsettling, yet strangely real
Boys of St Vincent weaves old-world guilt and folklore in a small Canadian town’s haunted alleys
Man Without a Face stranded in a dreary New Jersey inn, the story’s off-kilter vibe feels primal and uncanny
Princess Bride relocates fairy-tale strangeness to a remote kingdom, earthy and delightfully unsettling
